title: Review: Interior Gateway Protocols
author: Bhat, Lateef Ahmad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: An Interior Gateway Protocol (IGP) is a routing protocol which is used to exchange routing information within routing domain or autonomous systems. This paper consists of different classification of Interior gateway routing protocols. This paper also includes the comparison between IGRP, EIGRP, OSPF, protocols.
steps_raw: An Interior Gateway Protocol (IGP) is a routing protocol which is used to exchange routing information within routing domain or autonomous systems. This paper consists of different classification of Interior gateway routing protocols. This paper also includes the comparison between IGRP, EIGRP, OSPF, protocols.
keywords: IGP, Routing protocol, OSPF, EIGRP, IGRP, RIP, IS-IS.
